chemistry_overall: This SNAr reaction shows excellent chemistry fundamentals across all candidates: 0.15 M concentration is optimal for nucleophilic aromatic substitution, PFA material is fully compatible with DMF at 150°C with 110°C safety margin, and the air atmosphere is appropriate since no O₂-sensitive intermediates are involved. The key differentiator is residence time optimization - longer τ values (152.9 min) achieve higher conversions (X=0.70) while shorter times (72-73 min) sacrifice conversion for throughput. Since this is non-photochemical, photonics scores reflect engineering practicality where smaller diameters offer better heat transfer and mixing despite higher pressure drops.

chemistry_scores.reasoning: Strong chemistry performance with τ=127.3 min providing good X=0.63 conversion at optimal 0.15 M concentration. PFA material fully compatible with DMF at 150°C. The 0.75 mm diameter offers excellent heat transfer for this thermal SNAr reaction, though mixing is limited (r_mix=0.018). Air atmosphere is correct since SNAr generates no O₂-sensitive radicals. Low pressure drop (0.025 bar) ensures stable operation.
concerns: Low mixing number (0.018) may limit mass transfer in this bimolecular reaction

kinetics_overall: This nucleophilic aromatic substitution at 150°C shows concerning kinetics across all candidates. The estimated τ_kinetics is 48 min (center), but most candidates fall well below this threshold with τ values of 72-153 min. Conversions are critically low: X = 0.43-0.70, all below the 0.85 target. The intensification factors appear reasonable (3.3-15×) for thermal reactions, but the fundamental issue is insufficient residence time. All candidates require significant τ increases to achieve adequate conversion.

kinetics_scores.reasoning: τ = 127.3 min vs τ_kinetics = 48 min gives τ/τ_k = 2.65×, yielding X = 0.63 which is well below the 0.85 target. IF = 480/127.3 = 3.77× is at the low end for thermal reactions but acceptable. τ_mixing_required = negligible for this slow reaction. The τ/τ_k margin is adequate for RTD effects, but conversion is fundamentally too low. Final τ decision: max(48, 0, 24) = 48 min minimum required.
kinetics_scores.tau_vs_literature: τ=127.3 min vs τ_lit=48 min — 2.65× longer than kinetic requirement
concerns: Conversion X = 0.63 significantly below 0.85 target
Requires longer residence time for adequate conversion

fluidics_overall: All 12 candidates operate in excellent laminar regime (Re 2-7) with very low pressure drops (0.002-0.025 bar) providing >99% pump headroom. Mixing is adequate across all candidates with r_mix values 0.015-0.148, well below the 0.20 threshold. All candidates use syringe pumps (Q < 2 mL/min) with PFA tubing suitable for DMF at 150°C. Geometry is feasible with L ranging 12-19m requiring single or dual coil builds. The main trade-off is between small diameter candidates (0.75mm) offering better mixing but higher blockage risk at very low flow rates, versus larger diameters (1.6mm) with higher throughput but approaching mixing limits.

fluidics_scores.reasoning: Re=2.0 (excellent laminar), ΔP=0.025 bar giving 99.9% pump headroom, r_mix=0.018 (excellent mixing), L=19.0m (feasible dual coil). Flow rate 0.066 mL/min with 0.75mm diameter creates blockage risk for particulates. PFA tubing appropriate for DMF. No mixing concerns with dual criterion far from threshold.

safety_overall: This nucleophilic aromatic substitution at 150°C in DMF presents manageable thermal risks with Da_thermal << 0.1 for all candidates. DMF is a REACH SVHC reproductive toxin requiring closed-system handling and fume hood extraction. All candidates use PFA tubing which is fully compatible with DMF at 150°C (110°C safety margin). BPR requirement is minimal (0.6 bar) due to DMF's low vapor pressure, and all candidates operate at 10 bar providing excellent safety margin. No atmosphere isolation needed for single-stage air-compatible reaction.

safety_scores.reasoning: Da_thermal = 0.02 (isothermal, safe). BPR required 0.6 bar vs current 10 bar provides 16× safety margin. PFA compatible with DMF at 150°C per tool check (110°C margin). Single-stage air atmosphere eliminates isolation concerns. DMF reproductive toxicity managed through closed system design.
safety_scores.material_rationale: DMF solvent at 150°C, 10 bar pressure, pH ~7 — PFA fully compatible with 110°C temperature margin per compatibility check
safety_scores.isolation_method: N/A — single stage, air atmosphere throughout
hazard_flags: DMF — REACH SVHC reproductive toxin; use closed system, fume hood extraction, nitrile gloves
conditions: Maintain closed system integrity
Ensure fume hood extraction for DMF handling
